scope: Bundles two opt-in / off-by-default changes: (I1, v0.9.2) confidence-weighted ranking GUC; (D1, v0.9.3) base-rate-adjusted reinforce() defaults with GUC override. Neither change alters recall@k by default.
justification: Confidence GUC (I1): default=0.0 — zero additive term, byte-identical to 0.9.1 without SET. No recall-path scoring change by default; activation is explicit. Reinforce delta re-tune (D1): default deltas changed (+0.10→+0.02, -0.15→-0.12); confidence column is populated by reinforce() only and carries zero weight in recall_hybrid unless the I1 GUC is activated. Recall ranking is therefore unaffected by D1 defaults. The re-tune is validated analytically: the prior +0.10 delta caused confidence to saturate at the ceiling under typical usage, destroying discriminability; the new +0.02 restores it. Analytical gate is correct here: no scoring path changes by default.
scoring_impact: none (GUC default=0.0; reinforce confidence column carries zero weight in recall unless I1 GUC is set)
new_regress_tests: confidence_boost_guc (5 assertions: GUC default OFF, GUC ON ranking order, cold-start invariance, flag-OFF regression, spread amplification)
reinforce_delta_guc (8 assertions: default success delta, default failure delta, GUC override success, GUC override failure, clamp prevents overflow, batch form, RESET restores defaults)
verification: Local installcheck on PostgreSQL 17 + pgvector: all REGRESS tests pass including confidence_boost_guc and reinforce_delta_guc. Fresh-install (pgmnemo--0.9.3.sql) and upgrade chain (0.9.1→0.9.2→0.9.3 via two ALTER EXTENSION steps) both verified. Recall@k reference: v0.9.1 navigation_correctness gate (graph expansion 0→1 neighbor on semantic edge) carries forward unchanged.
